items.title: Trump admits using foul language in call with Netanyahu
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump has confirmed a tense exchange with Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in which he reportedly berated his close ally with expletives. In an interview published Wednesday in the New York Post, Trump was asked about the conversation he had with Netanyahu over the phone on Monday. "I did," Trump responded.

items.title: HR ministry continues to strengthen TVET ecosystem to meet global challenges - Ramanan
items.summary: Its minister, Datuk Seri R. Ramanan, said TVET is now among the country's strategic agenda and is no longer just a skills training system. "TVET is not just a skills training system, but the foundation for economic resilience, industrial strength and the future of the country's competitiveness," he said in a statement in conjunction with the 2026 National TVET Day celebration here today. He said the employability rate of graduates of training institutes under the ministry reached 95.5 per cent within six months of completing their training, with 85.1 per cent finding employment in fields related to their training.

items.title: RON97 drops 30 sen, RON95 and diesel down 20 sen
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The retail prices of RON95, RON97 petrol and diesel have been reduced for the June 4 to 10 period. The Finance Ministry said diesel in Peninsular Malaysia will be sold at RM4.67 per litre, down 20 sen from RM4.87 per litre previously. RON97 will be priced at RM4.35 per litre, down 30 sen from RM4.65, while RON95 will be sold at RM3.72 per litre, down 20 sen from RM3.92.

items.title: MACC launches 'Maccampus' application to strengthen anti-corruption education
items.summary: Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) deputy director (Community Education Division) Samsul Salip said that the application was developed as a learning platform that provides various information related to integrity, anti-corruption, and research for the benefit of university students. "Also included in the application is the sharing of research results conducted by the MACC in collaboration with universities. He said the new MACC Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman is scheduled to attend the programme, in collaboration with UMT, the Employees Provident Fund (EPF), the Credit Counselling and Management Agency (AKPK) and police.

items.title: MMEA detains two illegal passenger boats carrying 48 people
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) detained two passenger boats carrying 48 passengers and three crew members in Terengganu waters yesterday for operating without valid licences, including one vessel operated by an uncertified skipper. Terengganu MMEA Director Maritime Captain Hamiluddin Che Awang said the first boat was detained at 12.40pm at a position 27.6 nautical miles from the Kuala Terengganu estuary, while the second was detained at 1.37pm about 27.8 nautical miles from the same location. "The second boat was also found to be operating without a valid boat licence.

items.title: DKU secretary appointed by Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, says state secretary
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Negri Sembilan State Secretary Datuk Mohd Zafir Ibrahim has clarified that the appointment of the Dewan Keadilan dan Undang (DKU) secretary is made by the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir. He said the appointed individual will remain in office for as long as permitted by His Royal Highness, in accordance with Clause 25 of the Negri Sembilan Constitution 1959 (UUTKNS). "Therefore, no party other than the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan has the authority to suspend, terminate, replace or appoint any individual as the DKU secretary," he said in a statement here today.

items.title: Man jailed after hurling rocks at cars on Sprint Expressway, injuring one
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A man has been jailed for 26 months after a rock-throwing rampage near Jalan Setia Puspa here. Brickfields police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook said the 50-something suspect was seen at about 11am yesterday hurling rocks at passing vehicles along the Sprint Expressway, damaging six vehicles and injuring one victim. All six investigation papers were referred to the Kuala Lumpur deputy public prosecutor's office today, and the suspect was subsequently charged at the Kuala Lumpur magistrate's court under Section 427 of the Penal Code.

items.title: Lawyer seeks Federal Court’s leave to appeal over Agong’s refusal to declare emergency five years ago
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 3 — Lawyer Syed Iskandar Syed Jaafar Al Mahdzar has filed an application at the Federal Court seeking leave to appeal in relation to his judicial review challenge concerning the former Yang di-Pertuan Agong’s refusal to a request by the then prime minister to declare a state of emergency five years ago. When contacted by Bernama, Syed Iskandar confirmed that the notice of motion was filed last Friday through law firm Messrs R.Kengadharan & Co. On May 4 this year, the Court of Appeal three-man bench comprising Federal Court judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah and Court of Appeal judges Datuk Supang Lian and Datuk Dr Alwi Abdul Wahab, dismissed Syed Iskandar’s appeal. In dismissing Syed Iskandar’s appeal, Justice Sequerah ruled that matters concerning emergency powers under Article 150 of the Federal Constitution are non-justiciable.

items.title: Trump claims Iran has agreed to forgo nuclear weapons
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said Iran has agreed not to have a nuclear weapon and that he would probably meet with Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei at some point if things "work out." Asked about Khamenei's involvement in talks with the US on ending hostilities, Trump said, "He's involved, absolutely. "Iran's a big success," Trump said in the interview.

items.title: Science minister: Malaysia's gas supply sufficient, not overly dependent on external sources
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — Malaysia’s gas supply remains sufficient for now, with the majority of the country’s electricity generation supported by domestic sources, according to Science, Technology and Innovation Minister Datuk Chang Lih Kang. He said authorities continue to monitor developments in the industry. Our supply comes mainly from domestic sources and Australia.

items.title: Global supply chain risks put Malaysia-Indonesia strategic cooperation in focus
items.summary: Malaysian Embassy in Jakarta charge d'affaires Farzamie Sarkawi said the annual meeting comes at a crucial time as the region faces pressure on supply chains, trade flows and economic stability due to current global developments. "At a time when global supply disruptions are placing pressure on regional supply chains, strong bilateral relations between Malaysia and Indonesia are increasingly important," Farzamie said at the Malaysian Embassy in Jakarta today. Farzamie said energy security could also feature prominently in discussions, given the long-standing energy trade relationship between the two countries.

items.title: Trump wants to meet Iran leader Khamenei
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said he "would like to meet" Iran's supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei, in an interview published Wednesday. "I would like to meet him, and we probably will meet at some point, depending on how it all works out," Trump told the New York Post's "Pod Force One." --AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Iran attack on Kuwait airport leaves one dead, 63 injured
items.summary: KUWAIT CITY: An Iranian attack on Kuwait's airport wounded at least 63 people on Wednesday, the health ministry said, with authorities earlier reporting one person killed. Health ministry spokesman Abdullah al-Sanad said 25 ambulances were dispatched at Kuwait International Airport, adding that "63 injured individuals were received and distributed among hospitals... This includes serious injuries... including head wounds, cerebral hemorrhages, amputations and injuries resulting from explosions."

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 3, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The FBM KLCI closed lower, weighed down primarily by losses in the telecommunications and banking sectors. Investor sentiment shifted towards technology and semiconductor-related stocks, mirroring the strong rally in US equities, particularly chipmakers and artificial intelligence-linked companies. In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin fell to RM268,740 amid deteriorating market sentiment.

items.title: SAR operation for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin scaled down after 95pc of trail searched, say police
items.summary: Tapah district police chief Supt Abdul Malik Hasim said the decision was reached at a SAR coordination meeting chaired by Batang Padang district officer Ahmad Redhauddin this afternoon. Tapah district police chief Supt Abdul Malik Hasim said the decision to scale down the operation was made at a SAR coordination meeting this afternoon. Abdul Malik said the previous SAR operation, which lasted 11 days, involved a total of 116 personnel.

items.title: Zahid, Onn Hafiz to lead BN-Johor election machinery launch on June 7
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) will officially launch its election machinery for the upcoming state election at a major gathering expected to draw thousands of supporters at EduCity Stadium here on Sunday (June 7). The event will be jointly led by BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who is also the caretaker Johor Menteri Besar. Party promotional materials described the event as a platform to "mobilise, strengthen and energise" BN's election machinery across Johor constituencies, as BN prepares to defend its position in the state.

items.title: Coffee shop told to remove halal, non-halal signage after uproar
items.summary: Penang Island City Council (MBPP) public health committee alternate chairman Tan Soo Siang said the council had taken stern action against the coffee shop operator and issued a warning following public complaints. "MBPP instructed the coffee shop to remove the signage immediately and issued a warning to the operator. Tan said such actions could create unnecessary racial and religious sensitivities.

items.title: ‘Devoid of legal effect’: Negeri Sembilan DKU secretary rejects suspension amid royal crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — Dewan Keadilan dan Undang (DKU) secretary Raja Norazli Raja Nordin has reportedly rejected his indefinite suspension by several Negeri Sembilan ruling chiefs, saying the move had no legal effect. According to The Star , Raja Norazli said in a letter dated today that he was appointed by Yang di-Pertuan Besar Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir and remained bound to carry out the duties entrusted to him unless instructed otherwise by the state ruler. The six had on May 29 announced the purported indefinite suspension of Raja Norazli, accusing him of failing to convene a special DKU meeting to discuss the state’s ongoing royal and adat crisis.

items.title: Backbencher claims MyKhas access cut after attending Bersama event
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Another government backbencher has reportedly lost access to constituency funds after attending former PKR deputy president Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli's Parti Bersama Malaysia event. Petaling Jaya member of parliament Lee Chean Chung said he is seeking clarification from the government after his access to constituency allocations was reportedly blocked via the MyKhas portal. "I am still a member of parliament who supports the unity government.

items.title: Alliance Bank, 99 Speed Mart offer zero-interest instalment plan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Alliance Bank Malaysia Bhd has partnered with 99 Speed Mart Retail Holdings Bhd to offer a zero per cent instalment payment plan through 99 Speed Mart's online bulk purchasing platform. It allows Alliance Bank credit cardholders, including business credit cardholders, to convert eligible online purchases of RM500 and above into a three-month zero per cent instalment payment plan. "This timely financial solution empowers businesses with enhanced flexibility in managing cash flow, particularly in current uncertain times, reducing upfront payment burdens for bulk purchases and maintaining steady inventory turnover.

items.title: Deploying heavyweights to tough seats part of DAP culture, says Ong Kian Ming
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: DAP's long-standing election strategy is to deploy senior leaders into challenging constituencies to expand its footprint, said party member Dr Ong Kian Ming. The former deputy international trade and industry minister and Bangi member of parliament said vacating safe seats to contest marginal or difficult constituencies was aimed at strengthening the party's reach in new political terrain. Citing DAP stalwart Tan Sri Lim Kit Siang as an example, he said the veteran repeatedly moved to challenging seats, including Ipoh Timor in 2004 and Gelang Patah in 2013, winning both.

items.title: AirAsia Philippines ordered to cease ops over alleged unpaid airport fees [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines (CAAP) has ordered Philippines AirAsia Inc. to cease operations at all airports under its administration over alleged unpaid airport-related charges amounting to 271.94 million Philippine pesos. Business Times has seen a June 2 order issued by CAAP director-general Lt Gen Raul L Del Rosario directing the airline to "cease and desist from conducting operations in all airports operated and administered by the CAAP". However, CAAP said airline operations across all airports under its administration remained normal and uninterrupted.

items.title: Widow seeks court order for rider's earnings data in RM1mil dependency claim
items.summary: He said the part time job was in addition to Amirul's full time employment as a store assistant at a machinery company for 15 years. Amirul died in a drink driving crash along Jalan Raya Barat on March 29. Faizi said Nadia has already obtained complete payslips from Amirul's primary employer but still requires confirmation of income from his second job in order to establish his total annual earnings for dependency claim purposes.

items.title: B15 biodiesel rollout strengthens Malaysia's energy security, says minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Plantation and Commodities Minister Datuk Seri Dr Noraini Ahmad encouraged Malaysians to shift to the new B15 biodiesel, which was rolled out on June 1. She said the National Biodiesel Programme is supported by a nationwide network of 452 palm oil mills, 54 refineries, 19 biodiesel processing facilities and 34 blending depots. He also said Malaysia currently has 19 biodiesel production facilities generating approximately 1.5 million litres of oil per month.

items.title: Fire in Delhi hotel kills at least 21, police say
items.summary: NEW DELHI: At least 21 people were killed in a fire at a hotel in Delhi on Wednesday, police said, in one of the worst such incidents in the national capital in recent years. "People spread mattresses, and a woman from the third floor jumped on it with a little kid," witness Sher Khan said. [[nid:1454605]] The fire was extinguished around midday, a Reuters witness said.

items.title: Rohingya man held over fatal Marang crash
items.summary: Marang police chief Supt Mohd Sofian Redzuan said the crash occurred at about 6pm on May 27 at Km12.5 of the Kuala Terengganu–Kuantan road near Kampung Rusila. The case was initially investigated under Section 43(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for careless and inconsiderate driving. Police have also seized the Proton Saga under Section 64(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.

items.title: UK police review race plan amid anger over treatment of dying student
items.summary: LONDON: British police chiefs are reviewing anti-racism guidance following outrage over the treatment of a white student, who was handcuffed as he lay dying after being stabbed by a Sikh man, officials said Wednesday. Far-right figures have seized on the case of 18-year-old Henry Nowak, who was handcuffed and heard on police bodycam footage repeatedly telling officers he could not breathe as he lay mortally wounded in December. His attacker, Vickrum Digwa, however, lied and said he was the victim as Nowak had racially insulted him.

items.title: Cops to take statements from 14 hikers who trekked with Jaslinda
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police will record statements from all 14 hikers who participated in the Trans Spencer Chapman expedition with missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin as part of ongoing investigations into her disappearance. Tapah district police chief Superintendent Abdul Malik Hasim said investigators would obtain statements from all members of the hiking group to establish a clearer timeline of events. He said the exercise formed part of standard police procedures and was aimed at gathering all available information relating to Jaslinda's disappearance.

items.title: Online-bought soy milk maker explodes, injures mother and daughter in Penang
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A mother and her daughter suffered burn injuries after a soy milk maker purchased through a China-based e-commerce platform allegedly exploded during its first use at their home in Sungai Ara here yesterday. According to the organisation's advisor, John Ooh Sin Hwa, the victim, identified only as Madam Zhang, had purchased the appliance online because her daughter enjoys soy milk. "How can such electrical appliances, allegedly lacking the necessary safety certification, enter the local market and be sold directly to consumers through overseas e-commerce platforms?"

items.title: Hundreds flee as South Africa anti-migrant mobs go door-to-door
items.summary: GANSBAAI: Hundreds of foreigners fearing for their lives have taken shelter in community halls on South Africa's south coast, saying mobs of locals were going door-to-door telling them to leave the country. "They said 'you are a foreigner, you don't belong in South Africa, so you must go'," Mozambican Thomas Vincent Baloyi told AFP in Gansbaai, around 110 kilometres (70 miles) southeast of Cape Town. "I said, 'no, I got documents to be here in South Africa'.

items.title: Will SpaceX IPO make Elon Musk a trillionaire?
items.summary: Forbes magazine on Tuesday estimated Musk's net worth at nearly US$835 billion, as compared with $342 billion in its annual rich list in March 2025. The valuation of the company, founded in 2002, is expected to range from US$1.7 trillion to US$2 trillion, up from a range that topped out at US$1.5 trillion in March. On Tuesday, platforms for trading unlisted shares valued the group at about US$1.5 trillion, with an individual share going for about US$129 on Forge Global (up from US$53 in mid-December) and US$118 on Nasdaq Private Market.

items.title: Lembah Kiara Park RM6.5mil upgrade focused on infrastructure works - Yeoh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Upgrading works at Lembah Kiara Park costing RM6.5 million have been completed, with most of the allocation spent on rehabilitating the park's drainage system, retention ponds and electrical infrastructure. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said the recreational park, located within Bukit Kiara Federal Park, is managed by the National Landscape Department and not Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL). She said the remaining RM3.3 million was used for pond and drainage rehabilitation works, including the restoration of the upstream pond, sediment basin, streams and the park's main retention pond.

items.title: Ong Kian Ming suggests ‘warlords’ influenced Skudai seat controversy involving Marina Ibrahim
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — DAP’s Ong Kian Ming today suggested that local party “warlords” may have been among the factors behind the controversy surrounding Marina Ibrahim’s decision not to defend her Skudai seat in the upcoming state election. In a lengthy Facebook post, Ong said he believed there were broader considerations behind the proposal for Marina to leave Skudai and contest the more challenging Tiram seat in Pasir Gudang. The former Bangi MP also disclosed that Marina was not initially the preferred choice for Skudai when DAP was planning its candidate lineup ahead of the 2022 Johor state election.

items.title: Private infra, real estate capital to play larger financing role in AI data centre boom
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Private infrastructure and real estate capital are expected to play a larger role in financing the AI-driven data centre boom, as companies move beyond traditional forms of funding, Goldman Sachs said in a note on Tuesday. "Private infrastructure and real estate will play an even larger role in the years ahead," Goldman said. Private infrastructure's structured income generation and inflation-protection characteristics will likely boost further growth, the broking said.

items.title: Toronto home sales rise the most in 10 months, prices dip
items.summary: TORONTO: Greater Toronto Area home sales posted their biggest monthly gain in 10 months in May, helped by improved affordability, while prices resumed their recent decline, Toronto Regional Real Estate Board data showed on Wednesday. Seasonally adjusted sales increased 10 per cent last month from April to 5,364 units, marking the third straight month of gains and the largest since July last year. The board's home price index edged 0.2 per cent lower month-over-month, after seasonal adjustment, to C$927,800 (US$670,569.53).

items.title: Police record Azam Baki’s statement in two separate probes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — Police have recorded a statement from Tan Sri Azam Baki in connection with two separate investigations involving allegations of defamation, abuse of power, and criminal intimidation. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the statement was recorded under Section 112 of the Criminal Procedure Code. “The second investigation involves allegations of abuse of power and criminal intimidation against a member of the public identified as Albert Tei,” he said in a statement.

items.title: Another PKR lawmaker claims access blocked to constituency fund portal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — The Petaling Jaya parliamentary office has been unable to access the MyKhas system since May 29, 2026, its MP Lee Chean Chung said in a statement today, making him the second lawmaker allegedly barred from the portal that provides access to constituency development funds. Lee said he issued the public statement to seek clarification from Putrajaya, stressing that critical matters related to constituency allocations and Projek Mesra Rakyat (PMR) have been disrupted. Both Lee and Subang MP Wong Chen are known allies of former minister and PKR defector Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli.

items.title: Teacher pleads not guilty to seven sexual assault charges involving two students in Terengganu
items.summary: SETIU, June 3 — A male teacher pleaded not guilty at the Setiu Sessions Court today to seven charges of physical sexual assault against two male students since last May. Darusalam Ab Rahman, 42, made the plea after all the charges were read out before Judge Ahmad Fadhli Mahmud. The charges were framed under Section 12(1) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act (SOACA) 2017 and read together with Section 16(1) of the same Act, which provides for a prison sentence not exceeding five years and liability to whipping.

items.title: Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin wants Perlis MB given space to govern until end of assembly term
items.summary: KANGAR, June 3 — The Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, wants Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah to be given the space to continue leading the state administration until the end of the current Perlis State Assembly term without any interference. His Royal Highness said political stability is a crucial factor that will influence investors’ confidence to invest in the state. As such, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin called on all state assemblymen to set aside their egos and extend full and continuous cooperation to Abu Bakar for the benefit of the people and the state.

items.title: Lab assistant facing murder charge over Klang delivery rider’s death pleads not guilty to drug offence
items.summary: KLANG, June 3 — A laboratory assistant who was previously charged with killing a motorcyclist in a road crash changed his plea to not guilty on one drug-related charge in the Magistrate’s Court here today. R. Saktygaanapathy, 28, changed his plea after the amended charge was read out again before Magistrate Nor Azilah Mat. According to the amended charge, he allegedly administered tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) into his own body at the Narcotics Criminal Investigation Division Office, South Klang District Police Headquarters (IPD) at 3.30 pm on March 29, 2026.

items.title: Perlis ruler urges state to pursue high-impact investments, explore new revenue sources for sustainable growth
items.summary: KANGAR, June 3 — The Perlis state government must explore innovative approaches suited to local potential to address increasingly complex economic challenges, said the Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail. Also in attendance was the Raja Perempuan of Perlis, Tuanku Tengku Fauziah Tengku Abdul Rashid. The Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail, and the Raja Puan Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Dr Hajah Lailatul Shahreen Akashah Khalil, were also present.

items.title: GOF foils bid to smuggle exotic birds from Thailand, seizes parrots worth RM100,000
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 3 — Four exotic birds worth approximately RM100,000, believed to have been smuggled from Thailand, were seized by the General Operations Force (GOF) in an operation near the illegal Kak Nah jetty in Pengkalan Kubor, Tumpat, yesterday. GOF Southeast Brigade Commander SAC Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the seizure was carried out under Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan at about 7 pm after a Strike Force team detected a man unloading several cages on the Malaysian side of Sungai Golok. “Inspection of the abandoned cages found two Blue-and-Gold Macaws and two Blue-fronted Amazon parrots, believed to have been brought in without valid permits.

items.title: Perlis receives RM661.7m under 13MP as ruler calls for faster project implementation
items.summary: KANGAR, June 3 — Perlis has received an allocation of RM661.70 million under the Rolling Plan 1 (RP1) of the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP), with expenditure performance reaching RM87.46 million, or 13.22 per cent, as of last April. The Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, said the spending involved the implementation of 354 projects. “Under the Rolling Plan 5 (RP5) of the 12th Malaysia Plan (12MP), Perlis received an allocation of RM632.56 million involving 188 projects.

items.title: Chinese national pleads not guilty to murdering compatriot at Cheras shopping mall restaurant
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — A former restaurant waitress, a Chinese national, pleaded not guilty in the High Court here today to murdering her female fellow national by stabbing her multiple times at a restaurant in a shopping mall in Cheras two years ago. The woman was accused of murdering her colleague, cashier Zhang Wei, 34, at 4.30pm on November 29, 2024. Deputy public prosecutor (DPP) Faridah Nurdinie Bahrum appeared for the prosecution, while the accused was unrepresented.

items.title: Woman pleads guilty to culpable homicide over death of one of her newborn twins in Johor Bahru
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 3 — A 22-year-old woman pleaded guilty at the Sessions Court here today to a charge of culpable homicide over the death of one of her newborn twin daughters last month. According to the charge sheet, she was accused of causing the death of a baby girl with the intention of causing bodily injury likely to result in death. Under Section 304(a) of the Penal Code, bail may be granted at the discretion of the Sessions Court or High Court.

items.title: Man swept away in Sungai Pahang crossing found drowned
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 3 — A man believed to have been swept away by strong currents while attempting to cross Sungai Pahang at Kampung Peragap, Temerloh yesterday was found drowned today. A spokesman for the Pahang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) said the body of Samsul Azmi Ishury, 41, was discovered at 9.35am, approximately 2.2 kilometres from Kampung Loyang, Temerloh. Apart from the fire and rescue department, the operation also involved the police, the Malaysian Civil Defence Force (APM), and was assisted by local villagers.

items.title: Wet weather ahead: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms, heavy rain in five states until 3pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit five states until 3pm today, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). It said that the areas involved are Perlis, Penang, and Kedah (Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Yan, Pendang and Kuala Muda). “Similar weather conditions are also expected in Sarawak, involving Sibu (Sibu and Selangau) and Mukah (Tanjung Manis and Daro),” MetMalaysia said in a statement.

items.title: Azam Baki grilled for two hours at Bukit Aman over threat allegations by businessman Albert Tei
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — Former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki was at Bukit Aman police headquarters today to give a statement over allegations by businessman Albert Tei that he had been threatened. Azam said he arrived at about 9am and gave his full cooperation to investigators, Sinar Harian reported today. Azam said he denied all allegations made by Tei.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan MB granted audience with Tuanku Muhriz at Istana Hinggap
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 3 — Yang Dipertuan Besar Negeri Sembilan Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir today granted an audience to Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun at Istana Hinggap here. Speaking to reporters afterwards, Aminuddin said the audience was part of his regular weekly schedule ahead of chairing the State Executive Council meeting. “It is the usual Wednesday meeting,” he said briefly.

items.title: Man suffers 50pc burns after suspected LPG explosion in Alor Gajah house
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, June 3 — A man suffered burns to about 50 per cent of his body after being hit by an explosion believed to have been caused by a liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) leak at a house in Taman Baiduri, Kuala Ina here today. Melaka Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) zone 2 chief, Assistant Fire Superintendent Zulkhairani Ramli said his department received an emergency call about the incident at about 10.26 am before a team of personnel from the Alor Gajah Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) was dispatched to the location. He said that while the victim was inspecting, a sudden explosion occurred which caused him to suffer burn injuries and serious damage to the house.

items.title: Retired civil servant loses nearly RM98,000 in TikTok investment scam
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 3 — A retired civil servant here lost RM97,933 after being duped by a fraudulent online investment syndicate. Kuala Terengganu district police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said that on May 14, the 59-year-old victim came across an investment advertisement on the TikTok application offering lucrative returns within a short period. The victim then contacted the suspect via Telegram and agreed to make an initial investment using his pension funds.

items.title: Fadhlina: Budget 2027 cost-saving measures will not affect key education initiatives
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — Government cost-saving measures in drafting Budget 2027 will not affect the Ministry of Education’s (MOE) key plans, particularly in the recruitment of new teachers and the construction of additional classrooms, said Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek. In addition, Fadhlina said that repairs to dilapidated schools and building maintenance will continue as usual. Fadhlina said the MOE will also ensure that any cost-saving measures will not affect the rollout of the new curriculum next year, as well as the intake of six-year-old children into Year One.

items.title: Businessman pleads not guilty to dangerous driving charge over teen’s death in Kuala Terengganu crash
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 3 — A businessman pleaded not guilty in the Magistrate’s Court here today to a charge of dangerous driving that caused the death of a teenage boy in an accident at Jalan Losong Feri in May. Asrizal Hisham Khalid, 43, was charged with dangerous driving a Volkswagen GTI car that caused the death of Wah Han Keong, 18, at 3.36 pm, on May 19. In addition, the accused also faces a disqualification from holding or obtaining a driving licence for a period of not less than five years from the date of conviction.

items.title: Labuan MP urges landlords to vet tenants after 57 arrested in online scam raid
items.summary: LABUAN, June 3 — Labuan Member of Parliament Datuk Suhaili Abd Rahman has urged property owners on the duty-free island to exercise greater caution when renting out their premises to prevent them from being misused for online scam operations. He said the recent police raid at a commercial building in downtown Labuan, which led to the arrest of 57 individuals believed to be involved in an international investment scam syndicate, should serve as a lesson to all property owners. “The tenants had apparently misrepresented the purpose of renting the premises, claiming that the space would be used for agro-related investment activities.

items.title: Bersama to contest Johor polls, launches nationwide ‘Jelajah Kancil’ campaign tour
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) has confirmed it will contest the Johor state election in 2026, marking its first electoral outing since being revived on May 17. Alongside its electoral plans, Bersama will launch its nationwide campaign tour, Jelajah Kancil, beginning June 6 at the Danga Bay Convention Centre at 8pm. Stops include Melaka (June 7), Negeri Sembilan (June 13), Perak (June 14), Pahang and south Terengganu (June 21), north and central Selangor (June 28), south Selangor (July 1), Kelantan and north Terengganu (June 20), Pulau Pinang (July 4), Kedah and Perlis (July 5), Kuala Lumpur (Setiawangsa, July 11), Selangor Barat (Shah Alam, July 12) and Selangor Timur (Pandan, July 18).

items.title: Five, including baby girl, killed after family vehicle crashes into water pipe in Serian
items.summary: KUCHING, June 3 — Five people, including a two-year-old girl, were killed in a single-vehicle crash along Jalan Baki Lama-Ensengei in Serian this morning. According to the Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba), firefighters from the Serian fire station were dispatched to the scene after receiving an emergency call at 8.41am. The incident involved five victims understood to be from one family, comprising a man, three women aged between 16 and 47, and a two-year-old girl.

items.title: Tabung Haji: Concealing Zamzam water in bags may result in luggage being opened and delayed
items.summary: MAKKAH, June 3 — Carrying large quantities of Zamzam water as souvenirs may result in complications, with haj pilgrims’ luggage subject to detention or inspection by Saudi authorities. Haj Pilgrims Operations deputy director Mohd Ridzuan Yusoff said that despite annual reminders, some pilgrims still attempt to bring extra Zamzam water home by concealing it in their luggage. Mohd Ridzuan said a more concerning risk is that pilgrims’ personal belongings may be misplaced or damaged during the inspection process if their luggage has to be opened.

items.title: Fadillah: Strategic partnerships between govt, industry and financiers key to accelerating Malaysia’s energy transition
items.summary: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, said the successful convening of the second edition of Energy Transition Conference 2026 (ETCon26) by Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) is commendable as a significant achievement, as it has evolved into a credible platform that brings together leading minds from across the world to exchange ideas and drive progress in the energy sector. “The conference also reflects Malaysia’s strong commitment to accelerating the energy transition, signalling a decisive shift from ambition to execution as the country advances its efforts towards a more sustainable energy future,” he said at the Energy Transition Conference 2026 (ETCon26) here today. The first ETCon was held in conjunction with the National Energy Transition Roadmap in 2023.

items.title: Six shoplots razed in late-night fire in Tanjong Karang, no casualties reported
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 3 — Six shops, including a jewellery shop, were destroyed in a fire on Jalan Besar in Tanjong Karang near here last night. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said they were alerted to the incident at about 11.27 pm yesterday and dispatched personnel to the scene. “The operations commander reported that the fire involved six shoplots, each measuring 40ft by 20ft.

items.title: PLUS urges motorists to plan return journey using MyPLUS-TTA ahead of peak congestion from June 4–7
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 3 — Plus Malaysia Berhad (PLUS) is urging motorists returning to the Klang Valley after the Hari Raya Aidiladha and extended festive holidays to plan their journeys early using the MyPLUS-TTA digital travel schedule to avoid heavy congestion. The highway concessionaire said it expects a significant surge in traffic volume across its network during the return commute period, particularly on Peak Days identified as June 4, 5, 6 and 7, 2026. As part of its festive traffic management plan, PLUS said Smart Lane routes have been activated at 33 strategic locations along its highways.
